question: how do i get a bucket of water
answer: You can collect water by using a bucket on a water source block or a full water cauldron.

question: does depth strider actually make me faster in water
answer: Yes, the Depth Strider enchantment increases your water movement efficiency by 1/3. It also reduces the speed at which flowing water pushes you when you are standing still.

question: why does my water source keep disappearing when i try to make an infinite pool
answer: A water source block only forms if a flowing block is horizontally adjacent to two or more other source blocks and is sitting on top of a solid block or another water source. If you are in Java Edition, check if the game rule waterSourceConversion is set to false, as this disables the formation of new source blocks. Also, ensure your setup allows the flowing water to be adjacent to two existing sources to trigger the creation of a new one.

question: how do i get a bucket of lava
answer: You can collect lava by using a bucket on a lava source block or a full lava cauldron. This will turn your empty bucket into a lava bucket.

question: can i make infinite lava in survival
answer: Yes, you can make lava renewable by placing a lava source block above a pointed dripstone stalactite. If you place a cauldron within 10 blocks under the tip, there is a small chance it will slowly fill with lava, which you can then collect with a bucket.

question: why does lava burn me for so long after i get out
answer: When you touch lava, your remaining fire time is set to 300 ticks, which is 15 seconds. This timer resets every single tick you spend inside the lava, so it only starts counting down once you are completely out. You take damage 14 times while this timer ticks down, unless you jump into water or rain to put the fire out.

question: how do i make a villager get a job
answer: To give a villager a profession, you need to place a job site block, such as a lectern or composter, near an unemployed villager. In Java Edition, the villager must be able to pathfind to the block to claim it, while in Bedrock Edition, they can claim it within a 16-block radius.

question: can i change a villager's profession after i've already traded with them
answer: No, once you have made a trade with a villager, their profession is locked forever. If you haven't traded with them yet, you can change their profession by breaking their current job site block and placing a new one, provided they are not a nitwit.

question: why do my villagers keep losing their jobs and becoming unemployed
answer: Villagers lose their profession if they cannot reach their claimed job site block to work. In Java Edition, if a villager fails to reach their workstation after several attempts, they will eventually become unemployed. You should ensure the workstation is accessible and not blocked by other objects so the villager can pathfind to it and refresh their trades.

question: how do i make a pickaxe in minecraft
answer: you can craft a wooden, stone, iron, golden, copper, or diamond pickaxe by placing two sticks and three of the corresponding material in a crafting table.

question: can i repair my pickaxe in an anvil
answer: yes, you can repair a pickaxe in an anvil by using the same material it is made of. each unit of material you add will restore 25% of the pickaxe's maximum durability.

question: why did my pickaxe break instantly when mining gold ore
answer: if you use a pickaxe that is not the correct tier, it applies a penalty to the mining time and you will not get any drops. gold ore specifically requires an iron, diamond, or netherite pickaxe to be harvested successfully. if you use a wooden or stone pickaxe on it, the block will break but you won't receive the gold ore item.

question: how do i change my skin in minecraft
answer: In Java Edition, you can change your skin by uploading a PNG image file in the Minecraft Launcher or on the preferences page of minecraft.net. In Bedrock Edition, you can use the Character Creator menu found on the title screen or pause menu to customize your appearance.

question: does sprinting in water actually make you faster
answer: Yes, sprinting while submerged in water causes you to dive and swim significantly faster. However, if you sprint at the surface of the water, you will stay at the same altitude instead of swimming.

question: how does the game calculate my movement speed when walking or sprinting
answer: Your movement speed is determined by a base acceleration value that is countered by the friction of the block you are walking on. Every tick, your velocity increases by the acceleration amount and is then multiplied by the block's friction value, which is 0.546 for most blocks. This creates a balance where the friction eventually offsets the acceleration, resulting in a steady speed of 4.317 meters per second for walking and 5.612 meters per second for sprinting.

question: what do zombies drop when they die
answer: Zombies drop rotten flesh as their primary item. They also have a small chance to drop iron ingots, carrots, potatoes, or baked potatoes.

question: do baby zombies burn in the sun
answer: Yes, baby zombies burn when exposed to direct sunlight, just like adult zombies. They are also faster than adults and can fit through 1x1 block gaps.

question: how do i stop zombies from calling for help when i hit them
answer: In Java Edition, damaged zombies can call for reinforcements within a large area. This behavior is linked to the game difficulty; on Hard, they can even spawn extra zombies to help them. You cannot stop this behavior once it starts, but you can prevent it by setting the doMobSpawning game rule to false or by avoiding combat in areas where they can easily pathfind to you.

question: how do i download the bedrock dedicated server software?
answer: You can download the Bedrock Dedicated Server software free of charge from the official download page, which also lists the system requirements.

question: can i use the /reload command to update my server settings?
answer: No, the /reload command does not work for the Bedrock Dedicated Server. You must restart the server every time you change settings or add new plug-ins.

question: why does my server feel laggy even when cpu usage looks low?
answer: If your server is experiencing high load, such as from a large number of entities or complex chunks, it can become stuck even if CPU utilization appears low. The software performs poorly on multi-core server CPUs with low clock speeds, so high clock speed desktop processors are generally better for running the server.

question: how do i craft a chest in minecraft?
answer: You can craft a chest by placing any type of wooden planks into all eight outer slots of a crafting grid, leaving the center slot empty.

question: why wont my two chests turn into a large chest?
answer: Chests will not combine into a large chest if you are sneaking while placing the second one in Java Edition, or if you place the second chest facing a different direction. Additionally, trapped chests will never combine with normal chests.

question: what happens to the items if i break one half of a large chest?
answer: When you destroy one half of a large chest, the items stored in that specific half are dropped, and the remaining half continues to work as a small chest. In Java Edition, the top three rows of the left-hand chest or the bottom three for the right-hand chest are dropped. In Bedrock Edition, the items dropped depend on which half was placed first or last.

question: how do i make a skeleton stop burning in the sun
answer: Skeletons will stop burning if they are in water, in a shaded area with a light level of 11 or less, or if they are wearing a helmet. They also stop burning if there is a non-air block directly over them.

question: do skeletons shoot faster on hard mode
answer: Yes, skeletons shoot once every 2 seconds on Hard difficulty, whereas they shoot once every 3 seconds on Easy and Normal. Additionally, their accuracy is higher on Hard, with an error rating of 2 compared to 6 on Normal and 10 on Easy.

question: why do skeletons sometimes run away from me instead of shooting
answer: Skeletons will flee if they are being chased by a wolf, though they will turn to shoot if the wolf successfully damages them. In Java Edition, they also retreat to a safe range if you get within 4 blocks of them while strafing. If a skeleton has no bow and is forced to use a melee weapon, it will charge at you with its arms outstretched instead of using ranged attacks.

question: how do i make a village in minecraft
answer: You cannot manually craft or build a village from scratch to make it function as a natural one. Villages generate naturally in the world, and they only exist if there is at least one villager linked to a bed.

question: why are my villagers not restocking their trades
answer: Villagers need to be linked to their specific job site block to restock. If an employed villager is not linked to a job site block, they will be unable to restock their trades.

question: how does the game decide where the center of a village is
answer: In Bedrock Edition, the center is defined by the point of interest block linked to the first villager, and it can shift as villagers link to new beds or other blocks. In Java Edition, villages do not have a single defined center; instead, they are based on subchunks containing at least one claimed bed, bell, or job site block, with the village extending to a 3x3x3 cube of sections around those blocks.

question: how do i break stone in minecraft
answer: To break stone, you must use a pickaxe. If you try to break it with your bare hands or the wrong tool, it will take much longer and you will not get the block drop.

question: why is mining underwater so slow
answer: Mining underwater is slow because breaking a block takes 5 times as long if your head is submerged. If your feet are also not touching the ground, you get an additional 5x penalty, making you mine 25 times slower than on land. You can negate the head-submersion penalty by wearing a helmet with the Aqua Affinity enchantment.

question: how does instant mining work exactly
answer: Instant mining occurs when the damage a tool deals to a block in a single game tick exceeds 30 times the block's hardness. If your tool and enchantments meet this requirement, the block breaks with no delay. If they do not, there is a 6-tick (3/10 second) delay before you can start breaking the next block. For example, using Haste II with an Efficiency V diamond or netherite pickaxe allows you to instantly break stone.

question: how do i make a block of emerald?
answer: You can craft a block of emerald by placing 9 emeralds into a crafting grid.

question: can i use fortune on emerald ore?
answer: Yes, using a pickaxe enchanted with Fortune on emerald ore can increase the number of emeralds dropped. You can get up to a maximum of 4 emeralds with Fortune III, but if you use Silk Touch instead, the ore will drop as a block.

question: do i get more emeralds from vindicators if i use looting?
answer: Yes, using a weapon with Looting increases the emerald drops from vindicators. In Java Edition, the default drop chance is 50%, and using Looting III increases the average drop amount to 2.00. In Bedrock Edition, the drop rates and quantities vary significantly depending on the specific raid-spawned mob and the level of the enchantment applied.

question: how do i craft arrows in minecraft
answer: You can craft arrows by placing one flint, one stick, and one feather in a crafting grid. This recipe will yield four arrows.

question: why cant i pick up the arrows i just shot at my friend
answer: You cannot pick up arrows that are stuck in a player. Additionally, arrows shot by a bow with the Infinity enchantment or the extra arrows from a Multishot crossbow cannot be retrieved.

question: do arrows fired from a dispenser do less damage than ones from a bow
answer: Yes, arrows from a dispenser generally deal less damage. In Bedrock Edition, dispenser arrows do not scale damage with speed at all, resulting in an average of 3.5 HP. In contrast, a standard bow shot deals 8.5 HP on average, and this increases significantly if you use a Power enchantment, which adds 2.5 plus the power level to the damage tag.

question: how do i make planks in minecraft
answer: you can craft planks by placing any log, stem, stripped log, stripped stem, wood, or hyphae into a crafting grid. you can also craft them using a block of bamboo or a block of stripped bamboo.

question: can i use crimson planks as fuel in a furnace
answer: no, crimson and warped planks cannot be used as fuel in a furnace. you have to convert them into other items like sticks or tools instead.

question: why do my crimson and warped planks sound different than my oak ones
answer: nether planks, which include crimson and warped variants, have unique sounds compared to overworld wood. while overworld planks use the standard wood sound set, nether wood blocks have their own specific audio identifiers for breaking, placing, and walking, which is why they sound distinct when you interact with them.

question: how do i make a creeper explode on purpose?
answer: You can force a creeper to explode by using a flint and steel or a fire charge on it.

question: do creepers still explode if i hit them and run away?
answer: A creeper will only explode if it has an uninterrupted line of sight with you for the full 1.5-second countdown. If you move out of its sight or get more than 7 blocks away, the detonation will be canceled.

question: why did that creeper explode so fast when it fell on me?
answer: Creepers have a special behavior where taking fall damage adds to their internal swell timer. The amount added is equal to the fall distance multiplied by 1.5. If the creeper falls from a height greater than 16 blocks, it can trigger an explosion as soon as 5 ticks after landing.

question: how do i get wool from a sheep
answer: You can get 1–3 wool blocks by using shears on a sheep. This does not damage the sheep, and the wool will regrow after the sheep eats a grass block.

question: can i breed sheep with anything other than wheat
answer: No, sheep can only be bred using wheat. After they successfully breed, they cannot breed again for five minutes.

question: why did my sheep stop growing back its wool
answer: Sheep regrow their wool only after they graze on grass blocks, short grass, or ferns. If there is no grass available for the sheep to eat, it will remain sheared indefinitely. Additionally, if you have the gamerule mobGriefing set to false, the sheep will still regrow its wool even if it cannot eat the grass.

question: how do i craft a bed in minecraft?
answer: To craft a bed, you need to place three matching wool blocks in the top row of a crafting table and three wooden planks of any type in the middle row.

question: why cant i sleep in my bed even though it is night?
answer: You might be unable to sleep if there are monsters within 8 blocks horizontally or 5 blocks vertically of your bed. Additionally, if you are playing in Java Edition, you must be within 3 blocks of the bed to use it, or 2 blocks if you are in Bedrock Edition.

question: what happens if i try to sleep in the nether or the end?
answer: Attempting to sleep in the Nether or the End causes the bed to explode. The explosion has a power of 5, which is stronger than standard TNT, and it centers on the head part of the bed. This is why players often use beds as a cheap and instant way to mine ancient debris or damage bosses like the ender dragon.

question: how do i make a crafting table in minecraft?
answer: You can craft a crafting table by using four wooden planks in your inventory's 2x2 crafting grid.

question: can i play minecraft on my laptop and my friends play on their xbox?
answer: Yes, you can play together if you are both using Bedrock Edition. Bedrock Edition supports cross-platform multiplayer, allowing players on Windows, consoles, and mobile devices to join the same world.

question: why does my skin look weird or have holes in it when i play offline?
answer: In Minecraft, skins do not allow transparency on the base layer, so any pixels left empty in the skin file will appear as holes in your character model when you are playing offline. The second layer of a skin can be transparent, which is typically used for accessories like hats or glasses, but the main body layer must be solid color.

question: how do i tame a wolf in minecraft?
answer: You can tame a wolf by feeding it bones. Each bone has a 1/3 chance of successfully taming the wolf.

question: why wont my tamed wolf teleport to me?
answer: Tamed wolves will not teleport if they are sitting, attached to a fence with a lead, or if you are in a different dimension. They also require a specific 5x1x5 area with a solid block and air space to land on, so they may fail to teleport if they cannot find a valid spot near you.

question: do tamed wolves actually help with combat or do they just get in the way?
answer: Tamed wolves are quite helpful because they attack any mob or player that you attack, or that attacks you, provided the target is not on your same team. They deal 4 HP of damage regardless of the game difficulty. Additionally, kills made by your wolves count as player kills, which means the mobs they defeat can drop experience orbs and rare items that you would normally only get from your own attacks.

question: how do i turn a villager into a witch
answer: You can transform a villager into a witch by having it struck by lightning. This happens if lightning strikes within four blocks of the villager.

question: do witches despawn if they are in a swamp hut
answer: Witches that spawn inside a swamp hut during world generation will never despawn. However, any witches that spawn in the hut area after the world has already been generated can despawn normally.

question: why is it so hard to kill a witch with fire or drowning
answer: Witches are very difficult to kill with fire or water because they actively use defensive potions. If they are on fire, they have a 15% chance to drink a potion of Fire Resistance, and if they are underwater without the right effect, they have a 15% chance to drink a potion of Water Breathing. Additionally, they frequently drink potions of Healing whenever they are not at full health, which means you need to maintain constant damage output for 40 to 50 seconds to overcome their self-healing.

question: how many players can join a bedrock realms core server?
answer: A Realms Core subscription in Bedrock Edition allows for up to 4 players to be active in a world at the same time.

question: can i use experimental settings or mods on my realm?
answer: No, Realms cannot use experimental settings, mods, or plugins. However, you can bypass this for experimental settings by enabling them on a local world first and then uploading that world to your Realm.

question: why do i keep getting kicked from my realm after a while?
answer: Realms are set to automatically kick players after 15 minutes of inactivity. If you want to stay connected while away from your keyboard, some players use an endless circling pool to keep their character moving and prevent the server from detecting them as inactive.

question: how do i get leaf blocks in my inventory without them disappearing
answer: You can only obtain leaf blocks by breaking them with shears or a tool enchanted with Silk Touch. If you break them with any other tool, they will not drop as a block.

question: why do my leaves keep vanishing after i chop down the tree
answer: Leaves decay if they are not connected to a log or wood block. In Java Edition, they must be within 6 blocks of a log, while in Bedrock Edition, they must be within 4 blocks. However, if you place the leaves yourself, they will never decay.

question: does using fortune on leaves actually help get more saplings
answer: Yes, the Fortune enchantment increases the drop rates for saplings and sticks. For example, the base chance for a sapling from most leaves is 5%, but with Fortune III, that chance increases to 10%. Keep in mind that burning leaves will prevent them from dropping any saplings or apples regardless of your enchantments.

question: how do i make a boat in minecraft
answer: You can craft a boat by placing five matching Overworld planks in a U-shape in a crafting table.

question: can i put a chest on my boat and how
answer: Yes, you can create a boat with a chest by placing a chest and a matching boat together in a crafting grid. This allows you to transport items along with you while you travel.

question: why do my boats move so much faster on blue ice than on water
answer: Boats move faster on blue ice because the friction coefficient is significantly lower compared to water. While water has a friction value of 0.9, blue ice has a value of 0.989, which drastically reduces deceleration. This allows the boat to reach a top speed of over 72 blocks per second on blue ice, whereas it only reaches 8 blocks per second on water.

question: how do i breed pigs in minecraft
answer: You can breed pigs by feeding them carrots, potatoes, or beetroots. Once fed, they will enter love mode and produce a baby pig.

question: what happens if i ride a pig into deep water
answer: While you can control a pig through 1-block-high water using a carrot on a stick, entering deeper water will cause you to automatically dismount the pig.

question: how does the speed boost work when riding a pig
answer: When you use a carrot on a stick while riding a saddled pig, it consumes 7 durability and triggers a boost. The pig will gradually accelerate from its default speed of 2.42 blocks/s up to a peak of 5.2 blocks/s, then slowly decelerate back to normal speed by the end of the boost cycle. The average speed during this entire process is roughly 4.19 m/s.

question: how do i make an iron ingot
answer: You can obtain iron ingots by smelting iron ore or raw iron in a furnace or blast furnace. You can also craft them by placing 9 iron nuggets in a crafting grid.

question: do iron golems drop more iron if i use a looting sword
answer: No, iron golems always drop 3–5 iron ingots regardless of the looting enchantment level. The looting enchantment only increases the drop rates for other mobs like zombies or husks.

question: is there a way to fix my iron armor without using more ingots
answer: Iron ingots are the specific repair items for iron armor, so you must use them in an anvil to restore durability. You can also combine two damaged pieces of the same type in an anvil or crafting grid to combine their remaining durability plus a 5% bonus, but this does not use ingots.

question: how do i make planks in minecraft
answer: you can craft planks by placing any log or stem into a crafting grid. one log or stem will produce four planks.

question: can i use stems as fuel in my furnace
answer: no, stems cannot be used as fuel in a furnace. only logs can be used as fuel, and they will smelt 1.5 items per block.

question: is there any way to get charcoal without using logs
answer: no, you must use logs to create charcoal. you need to place any log into a furnace with fuel to smelt it. note that stems cannot be smelted into charcoal, so you must stick to using standard tree logs.

question: how do i make sticks in minecraft
answer: You can craft sticks by placing two planks in a vertical column in your crafting grid. You can also craft them using bamboo, where two bamboo items will yield one stick.

question: do i get more sticks from leaves if i use fortune
answer: Yes, using a tool with Fortune increases the drop rate of sticks from leaves. While a normal tool has a 2% chance to drop 1–2 sticks, Fortune I, II, and III increase those chances to 2.2%, 2.5%, and 3.3% respectively.

question: is it worth trading sticks to fletcher villagers for emeralds
answer: Trading sticks is a very common way to get emeralds because fletcher villagers will buy 32 sticks for one emerald. Since sticks are easily renewable by chopping wood or harvesting bamboo, this is a highly efficient way to build up your emerald supply. Just keep in mind that the trade has a price multiplier, so the cost can fluctuate depending on how often you use that specific trade.

question: how do i get ender pearls from endermen
answer: You get ender pearls by killing endermen. They have a 50% chance to drop one when killed, and you can increase the amount they drop by using a weapon with the Looting enchantment.

question: why do endermen keep teleporting away when i try to hit them
answer: Endermen automatically teleport when they take damage from a player or mob, or when they are hit by a projectile. If you are in Java Edition, they will often teleport to a spot behind you to counterattack, making them difficult to hit in a straight fight.

question: can i stop endermen from stealing my blocks
answer: Yes, you can prevent endermen from picking up or placing blocks by setting the game rule mobGriefing to false. If you want to keep the rule on, be aware that they only pick up specific blocks from a list, such as dirt, sand, or gravel, and they cannot pick up blocks from a completely flat floor unless it is made of non-full blocks like soul sand or mud.

question: how do i make a furnace in minecraft
answer: You can craft a furnace by placing eight cobblestone blocks in a crafting table, leaving the center square empty. Alternatively, you can simply pick up a furnace from a crafting table output if you have the materials ready.

question: why does my cobblestone not drop when i mine it
answer: Cobblestone must be broken using a pickaxe. If you use the wrong tool, the breaking time will increase and the block will not drop anything.

question: is it possible to make infinite cobblestone
answer: Yes, you can create a renewable source of cobblestone by managing how water and lava interact. When flowing water comes into contact with flowing lava, the lava is replaced by cobblestone. You must ensure the lava is flowing, because if a lava source block touches water, it turns into obsidian instead.

question: how do i stop piglins from attacking me in the nether
answer: You can stop piglins from being hostile by wearing at least one piece of golden armor. If you are already being attacked, you can sometimes distract them by throwing a gold ingot at them.

question: why did the piglin stop bartering with me and start attacking
answer: Piglins will stop bartering and become aggressive if you attack them, break gold-related blocks, or open containers like chests or barrels near them. If they are under 'heavy aggravation' from being damaged, they will refuse to barter and stay hostile for 30 seconds.

question: do piglins zombify if i take them to the overworld
answer: Yes, piglins will transform into zombified piglins after 15 seconds of being in the Overworld or the End. They will keep their armor and items, but they lose the ability to use crossbows for shooting and will use them as melee weapons instead. You can prevent this by setting their IsImmuneToZombification tag to true if you are using commands.

question: how do i craft a slab in minecraft?
answer: The page does not provide specific crafting recipes for slabs, but it notes that slabs are blocks with non-standard heights that occupy a partial cell in the 3-dimensional grid.

question: can i walk up a block that is taller than one meter?
answer: You can automatically step up from a lower to a higher height if the difference is at most 0.6 of a block. Since most solid blocks are 1 meter high, you cannot walk up a full block height without jumping.

question: why do some blocks like sand fall down but others just stay floating in the air?
answer: Most blocks in the game ignore gravity and stay where they are placed. However, specific blocks like sand, red sand, gravel, anvils, dragon eggs, concrete powder, scaffolding, pointed dripstone, and suspicious sand are programmed to turn into entities when their support is removed, which causes them to fall.

question: how do i spawn the wither
answer: To spawn the wither, place four blocks of soul sand or soul soil in a T-shape, then place three wither skeleton skulls on the top three blocks. The last block you place must be one of the skulls.

question: can i use soul soil instead of soul sand to make a wither
answer: Yes, you can use soul soil interchangeably with soul sand to build the T-shaped structure. Just make sure you have air blocks on either side of the base blocks before placing the final skull.

question: why does the wither stop taking damage from my arrows halfway through the fight
answer: When the wither drops below half of its total health, it gains a natural armor effect that makes it immune to projectiles like arrows and thrown tridents. This effect stays active until the wither regenerates back above half health. While this armor is active, you will need to use other methods, such as melee attacks or non-projectile damage sources, to continue hurting it.

question: how do i turn on subtitles in java edition
answer: You can enable closed captions in Java Edition by going to the 'Accessibility' section of the options menu or through the 'Music & Sounds' options.

question: what do the arrows mean next to the text in closed captions
answer: The arrows, specifically '<' or '>', appear next to the sound text to indicate the direction the sound is coming from if the source is off-screen. As the sound fades away or you move further from the source, the text will also fade and become less white.

question: why do some sounds like footsteps not show the entity name in subtitles
answer: While most entity sounds display the entity name followed by a specific verb, such as 'Rabbit squeaks', the game does not specify the object for certain actions. Specifically, the closed captions for removing or placing blocks and the sound of footsteps are generic and do not list the entity or block type responsible for the sound.

question: how do i make green dye in minecraft
answer: You can obtain green dye by smelting a cactus in a furnace with any fuel source.

question: can i redye terracotta or concrete once it is already colored
answer: No, stained terracotta and concrete cannot be redyed to a different color once they have been crafted.

question: why does my sheep keep changing color when i breed it
answer: When you breed two colored sheep, the lamb will be born as one of the parent colors, or a mix of the two. If the colors cannot be combined, the game randomly picks one of the two parent colors with an equal chance. This is why you might see different results than expected when breeding sheep of different colors.

question: how do i cure a zombie villager
answer: To cure a zombie villager, you must first apply the Weakness effect to it using a splash potion, lingering potion, or a tipped arrow. Once it is weakened, use a golden apple on the zombie villager to begin the curing process.

question: does the curing process go faster if i put stuff around the zombie villager
answer: Yes, you can speed up the curing process by placing iron bars or beds within a 9x9x9 cube centered on the zombie villager. Each of these blocks found, up to a maximum of 14, provides a 30% chance to decrease the countdown timer by one tick.

question: why do some zombie villagers turn into normal ones when i hit them with a sword
answer: If you are playing on Normal or Hard difficulty, any zombie that kills a villager has a chance to turn that villager into a zombie villager. On Normal difficulty, this chance is 50%, while on Hard difficulty, it is guaranteed at 100%. If you are seeing villagers turn into zombies during a fight, it is likely because a nearby zombie killed them.

question: how do i craft a dispenser
answer: You can craft a dispenser by placing one bow and one redstone dust around seven cobblestone blocks in a crafting table. The bow can have any amount of durability left, but enchantments on it will not carry over to the dispenser.

question: do i need a pickaxe to pick up a dispenser after i place it
answer: Yes, you should use a pickaxe to break the dispenser if you want to keep it. If you break it without using a pickaxe, the dispenser will be destroyed and you will only get the items that were stored inside it.

question: why is my dispenser not firing items immediately when i power it
answer: Dispensers have a built-in delay of 4 game ticks, which is about 0.2 seconds, between receiving a signal and actually ejecting an item. Also, remember that a dispenser only fires once on the rising edge of a redstone signal, so it will not keep shooting if you just leave it powered. If you want it to keep firing, you need to set up a clock circuit to repeatedly turn the signal on and off.

question: how do i make an axe in minecraft?
answer: You can craft an axe by placing 3 units of the tool material (like wood planks, stone, or iron ingots) and 2 sticks in a crafting table. The recipe can be arranged in a few ways as long as the materials are placed correctly.

question: why does my axe sometimes not strip logs when i have a shield in my off-hand?
answer: In Java Edition, holding a shield in your off-hand prioritizes blocking over stripping logs or scraping copper. To strip or scrape blocks while holding a shield, you need to sneak while using the axe.

question: does using an axe to break a block always use up durability?
answer: No, it depends on the block type. An axe consumes 1 durability point for every block it breaks that takes time to mine. However, if you use an axe on a block that breaks instantly, it does not consume any durability at all.

question: how do i craft a sign in minecraft?
answer: You can craft signs by combining matching planks and a stick in a crafting table. This recipe will yield three signs.

question: can i stop people from editing my signs on a server?
answer: Yes, you can prevent other players from editing your signs by using a honeycomb on them. Once a sign is waxed this way, it cannot be edited or unwaxed unless you break it and place it down again.

question: why does my sign text look different when i face east vs north?
answer: Signs facing east or west appear brighter and more saturated because Minecraft uses side lighting to prevent the world from looking flat. However, the sign itself is actually dimmer in those directions, and since the text on signs is not affected by the lighting engine, the visual contrast changes depending on the direction the sign is placed.

question: how do i craft a piston in minecraft
answer: To craft a piston, you need to combine any type of wooden planks, cobblestone, an iron ingot, and redstone dust in a crafting table.

question: can pistons push chests or other storage blocks
answer: In Java Edition, pistons cannot push blocks that hold block entities, such as chests, hoppers, or furnaces. However, in Bedrock Edition, these blocks can be pushed by pistons.

question: why do my pistons sometimes take longer to activate than others
answer: Pistons have a start delay of 2 game ticks in Bedrock Edition. In Java Edition, the delay is either 0 or 1 tick depending on when the piston receives power during the game's internal processes. If a piston is powered during the entity or block entity phase, it activates in the next tick, but if it is powered during the scheduled or random tick phases, it activates in the same tick.

question: how do i make a potion in minecraft
answer: you need to use a brewing stand. every potion starts with a water bottle, which you then brew with ingredients to get different effects.

question: what happens if i drink a potion while i already have the effect
answer: drinking a potion while you are already under the same effect will not add to the total time. instead, it just resets the duration back to the full amount.

question: does adding glowstone or redstone to a potion change how long it lasts
answer: yes, adding redstone dust extends the duration of the effect, while adding glowstone dust enhances the potency of the effect. these two modifications are mutually exclusive, meaning you cannot do both to the same potion. for example, enhancing a potion usually trades some of its duration for a stronger effect, while extending a potion makes it last 8/3 times longer than the base version.

question: how do i put out a fire in minecraft
answer: You can extinguish fire by using water, a splash water bottle, or a cauldron containing water. You can also put it out by placing blocks on top of it or by hitting the side of the burning block.

question: why do i keep taking damage even after i walk out of the fire
answer: When you stand in fire, you gain a 'Fire' value that tracks how long you will continue to burn. If you stay in the fire long enough to make this value greater than zero, you will continue to take damage for 160 game ticks after leaving the fire source.

question: does fire spread through cobblestone walls to my wooden roof
answer: Yes, fire can spread to flammable blocks even if there are non-flammable blocks like cobblestone in the way. Fire can turn air blocks into fire blocks up to one block downward, one block sideways, and four blocks upward from the original fire source, and it ignores non-flammable blocks that are physically between the fire and the target flammable block.

question: how do i breed chickens in minecraft
answer: You can breed chickens by feeding them wheat seeds, beetroot seeds, melon seeds, pumpkin seeds, torchflower seeds, or pitcher pods. Once you feed two adult chickens, they will enter love mode and produce a chick, which also grants you 1–7 experience orbs.

question: why do my chickens keep laying different colored eggs
answer: The color of the egg a chicken lays depends on its temperature variant, which is determined by the biome where it spawned. Temperate chickens lay regular white eggs, warm chickens lay brown eggs, and cold chickens lay blue eggs.

question: if i throw a stack of eggs, what are the actual odds of getting a bunch of chicks
answer: Every egg you throw has a 1/8 chance to spawn a single chick. If that spawn succeeds, there is an additional 1/32 chance that three extra chicks will appear alongside it. This means there is an overall 1/256 chance for a single thrown egg to result in four chicks total.

question: how do i tame a horse in minecraft?
answer: To tame a horse, you need to mount it repeatedly by pressing the use button with an empty hand until it stops bucking you off and hearts appear above it. You can also feed the horse food to increase its temper, which makes it easier to tame.

question: can i put armor on a baby horse?
answer: No, foals cannot be equipped with armor or a saddle because they cannot be ridden. You must wait for the foal to mature into an adult horse before you can add any equipment to it.

question: how does breeding affect the stats of a foal?
answer: When you breed two horses, the baby's health, movement speed, and jump height are calculated using a formula based on the parents' values. It takes the absolute difference of the parents' stats, adds 30% of the total allowed range for that stat, and applies a random distribution factor before averaging the parents' values. This means the offspring's performance is not just a simple average but a variation that can potentially result in better or worse stats than the parents.

question: how do i make a furnace
answer: You can craft a furnace by placing eight stone-tier blocks, such as cobblestone, blackstone, or cobbled deepslate, in a crafting grid, leaving the center slot empty.

question: do i lose my items if i break a furnace
answer: No, you do not lose your items. If you break a furnace, it will drop its contents, including any items currently being processed and any experience points that were accumulated from those items.

question: why does my coal keep burning even when the furnace is empty
answer: Once a piece of fuel starts burning, it continues to burn until it is fully consumed, even if the upper slot is empty or the item finishes processing early. This means if you pull your items out before the fuel is finished, the remaining burn time is wasted. To avoid this, try to keep the upper slot filled so the furnace can continue processing items without stopping.

question: how do i craft a torch
answer: You can craft torches by placing one coal or charcoal above one stick in a crafting grid. This recipe will give you 4 torches.

question: do torches melt ice and snow
answer: Yes, torches emit a light level of 14 and will melt snow layers within 2 blocks and ice within 3 blocks. This distance is measured using taxicab distance.

question: what happens to falling sand if it hits a torch
answer: Gravity-affected blocks like sand and gravel will not start falling if the space immediately beneath them is occupied by a torch. If a block is already falling and lands on a solid block that has a torch sitting on top of it, the falling block will break. However, if there is only an air block under the torch, the falling block will pass right through the torch and land on the surface below.

question: how do i make an anvil in minecraft?
answer: To craft an anvil, you need 31 iron ingots in total. You must first craft three blocks of iron using 27 of those ingots, then combine those three blocks with one additional iron ingot in a crafting table.

question: can i repair my diamond pickaxe with just diamonds?
answer: Yes, you can repair a single diamond item using diamonds in an anvil. Each diamond restores 25% of the item's maximum durability, but keep in mind that this is often less economical than combining two damaged tools, as it can cost up to four diamonds for a full repair.

question: why does my anvil keep saying it is too expensive when i try to combine enchantments?
answer: Anvils have a hard limit of 39 experience levels for any operation. Every time you repair or combine items, the cost doubles, and there is also a prior-work penalty that increases with each use. Once the total cost of the operation exceeds 39 levels, the anvil will refuse to perform the action, though this limit does not apply if you are playing in Creative mode.

question: do baby animals drop xp when you kill them?
answer: No, baby animals do not drop any experience orbs when killed, with the exception of baby hoglins.

question: if i use a silk touch pickaxe on diamond ore do i get the xp?
answer: No, you will not get experience if you mine diamond ore with a Silk Touch pickaxe. However, you can place the ore block down later and mine it without Silk Touch to release the experience and the mineral.

question: how does the game decide which size experience orbs to drop when i kill something?
answer: When you kill a mob or break a block, the game calculates the total experience amount and then splits it into specific orb sizes. It prioritizes the largest possible orb values first, using the set {1, 3, 7, 17, 37, 73, 149, 307, 617, 1237, 2477}. For example, if you were to drop 1000 experience, the game would drop orbs with values of 617, 307, 73, and 3.

question: do spiders drop anything when they die?
answer: Yes, spiders drop string and spider eyes when killed. They also drop 5 experience orbs if killed by a player or a tamed wolf.

question: why do spiders sometimes have potion effects on them?
answer: In Java Edition on Hard difficulty, spiders have a chance to spawn with Speed, Strength, Regeneration, or Invisibility effects. This happens during a pack spawn, and the effect is applied to all spiders in that group permanently.

question: how does light level affect spider aggression?
answer: Spiders are neutral and will not attack unless provoked if the light level immediately around them is above 11. If the light level is 11 or less, they become hostile toward players and iron golems on sight. If a spider is already chasing you and enters a brighter area, it will return to a neutral state, but it will stay aggressive if it is currently retaliating against an attack.

question: how do i check my current pack format in game?
answer: You can find your current pack format by typing /version in the chat or by pressing the F3 + V debug hotkey.

question: why does my resource pack say it is incompatible?
answer: A resource pack will show as incompatible if the format number defined in its pack.mcmeta file is higher than the version the game currently supports. This system is in place to prevent crashes or missing content that could occur if a pack uses features not available in your version of the game.

question: i noticed my pack.mcmeta file uses min_format and max_format instead of pack_format, is that normal?
answer: Yes, that is the new standard. Starting with snapshot 25w31a, the game moved away from the single pack_format field in favor of min_format and max_format fields. This allows creators to build a single pack that remains compatible across a specific range of game versions rather than being locked to just one.

question: how do i make glass in minecraft
answer: You can make glass by smelting sand in a furnace using any fuel source.

question: what happens if sand falls on a torch
answer: If sand falls onto a non-solid block like a torch, rail, or slab, it will break and drop as an item instead of placing itself as a block. This is a common way to quickly clear out large stacks of sand.

question: is there any way to get sand without mining it in a desert
answer: Yes, sand is a renewable resource. You can trade emeralds with wandering traders to get sand, or you can get it as mob loot from endermen who are holding a sand block. Additionally, in some versions, you can find it in desert pyramid chests, though trading is the only way to generate it infinitely.

question: how do i make a minecart
answer: You can craft a minecart by placing five iron ingots in a U-shape in a crafting table.

question: how do i get out of a minecart
answer: To exit a minecart, you need to press the sneak key while you are riding in it. When you dismount, the game will try to find a safe spot one block away to place you, checking horizontally first and then vertically.

question: why do my minecarts stop moving when i go far away
answer: Minecarts become frozen in place once they travel more than one chunk beyond your current simulation distance. They will only resume their movement once you get back within that range, as the game stops processing their motion to save resources while they are unloaded.

question: how do i get slimeballs in minecraft
answer: You can get slimeballs by killing slimes. They drop 0–2 slimeballs when killed, and you can increase this amount by using a sword enchanted with Looting.

question: why wont slimes spawn in my swamp base
answer: Slime spawning in swamps depends on the moon phase; they never spawn on a new moon and appear most often on a full moon. Additionally, they only spawn between Y=51 and Y=69 when the light level is 7 or less.

question: do slime chunks work the same on bedrock and java
answer: No, they function differently. In Java Edition, slime chunks are determined pseudo-randomly based on the world seed combined with chunk coordinates. In Bedrock Edition, the algorithm does not use the world seed, meaning slime chunks are located at the exact same coordinates in every single world.

question: how do i change the yellow text on the main menu?
answer: You can modify the splash texts by editing the respective text file (splashes.txt in Java Edition or splashes.json in Bedrock Edition) within a resource pack.

question: does the splash text change depending on what language i have selected?
answer: No, splash texts are not translated and will appear the same regardless of the language setting chosen in the game.

question: how fast does the splash text move on the title screen?
answer: The splash text pulsates on the title screen at a rate of twice per second, which is equivalent to 120 times per minute.

question: how do i find a trial chamber in my world?
answer: You can find a trial chamber by purchasing a trial explorer map from a journeyman-level cartographer villager for 12 emeralds and a compass. These maps will point you to the nearest trial chamber.

question: do trial spawners always spawn the same mobs?
answer: No, the mobs are not fixed. When a trial chamber structure generates, one mob category is randomly selected for the spawners in that structure, and every mob within that category has an equal chance to be chosen.

question: why are some trial spawners in my chamber not spawning breezes?
answer: Breezes are in their own unique category and are only guaranteed in certain chambers. Other trial spawners in the structure are randomly assigned a category like melee, small melee, or ranged, and those will never spawn breezes regardless of the chamber type.

question: how do i make a vindicator attack everything
answer: You can make a vindicator hostile to almost all mobs by naming it "Johnny" with a name tag or by setting its Johnny tag to 1b. This causes it to attack players and most mobs, though it will still flee from creakings.

question: do vindicators break doors in survival
answer: Vindicators can break wooden doors, but only if they are part of a raid and the game is set to Normal or Hard difficulty. In Java Edition, they can also open wooden and copper doors just like villagers.

question: why do my shields keep breaking when fighting vindicators
answer: Vindicators use an iron axe to attack, and their axe strikes have the ability to disable your shield for 5 seconds. This effect happens even if the vindicator is riding a ravager, making them one of the most dangerous melee mobs in the game. Because they deal high damage—up to 19.5 HP on Hard difficulty—you should be careful when approaching them without a way to mitigate that shield cooldown.

question: how do i make coarse dirt
answer: you can craft coarse dirt by combining two dirt blocks and two gravel blocks in a crafting grid.

question: does silk touch keep gravel from dropping flint
answer: yes, if you use a tool enchanted with silk touch, the gravel block will drop itself instead of flint. normally, breaking gravel without silk touch gives you a chance to get flint, but silk touch overrides this behavior.

question: why does my gravel break into an item when it lands on a torch
answer: gravel is a gravity-affected block that turns into an entity when the block beneath it is removed. if that falling entity lands in a space occupied by a non-solid block like a torch, rail, or slab, it cannot turn back into a solid block and will break into an item instead. because it breaks this way, it will never drop flint when landing on these types of blocks.

question: how do i get bad omen to start a raid
answer: You need to drink an ominous bottle, which you can get by killing a pillager captain. The effect lasts for 100 minutes, and once you enter a village, it will convert to raid omen to start the event.

question: do pillagers drop their crossbows when they die
answer: Yes, but only with an 8.5% chance when killed by a player or a tamed wolf. You can increase this drop chance by 1% for every level of the looting enchantment you have on your weapon.

question: why do my pillagers shoot arrows so fast when i summon them without a crossbow
answer: In Bedrock Edition, pillagers have both a ranged attack and a charge behavior in their code. If you summon one without a crossbow, the charge behavior fails to trigger, causing the game to default to the ranged attack component which fires arrows much more rapidly than a standard pillager would.

question: how do i turn a zombie into a drowned?
answer: You need to keep a normal zombie submerged in water continuously for 30 seconds. After that time, it will start shaking for 15 seconds before fully converting into a drowned.

question: do drowned spawn in rivers during the day?
answer: Yes, unlike in ocean biomes where they only spawn at Y < 58, drowned can spawn in rivers during the day as long as the water is deep enough for the sky light level to reach 7 or below.

question: why are my drowned farms not giving me tridents?
answer: To get tridents, your farm must be designed to harvest naturally-spawned drowned, as zombies converted into drowned will never be equipped with tridents. Additionally, only drowned that spawn with a trident in their main hand have a chance to drop it, and you should use a weapon with the Looting enchantment to increase your odds, as the base drop chance is quite low.

question: how do i get wool from a sheep
answer: You can get 1–3 wool by using shears on an adult sheep. If you don't have shears, you can also get 1 wool by killing the sheep.

question: does fortune or looting make sheep drop more wool
answer: No, neither the Fortune nor the Looting enchantments affect the amount of wool you get from sheep. Shearing an adult sheep will always yield 1–3 wool regardless of your gear.

question: why is my sculk sensor not picking up the warden or player movements
answer: Sculk sensors cannot detect vibrations if a wool block is placed between the sensor and the source of the vibration. Additionally, sensors are unable to detect entities walking, sprinting, or jumping directly on top of wool, nor can they detect the act of placing or breaking wool itself. This same principle applies to the warden, which is also unable to detect these specific wool-related actions.

question: why do i get cobblestone when i mine stone?
answer: Mining stone with a pickaxe will always drop cobblestone. If you want to get the stone block itself, you need to use a pickaxe enchanted with Silk Touch.

question: how do i make stone if i dont have any left?
answer: You can create stone by letting lava flow on top of water blocks. You can also get stone by smelting cobblestone in a furnace using any fuel.

question: is there a way to get stone without it turning into deepslate when i dig down?
answer: Stone naturally generates in the Overworld above Y=0, but it starts to transition into deepslate starting at Y=8. By the time you reach Y=0 and below, all the stone is completely replaced by deepslate. If you are mining for stone, you should stay above Y=8 to ensure you are finding stone rather than deepslate.

question: how do i make a cauldron
answer: You can craft a cauldron using seven iron ingots arranged in a U-shape in a crafting table.

question: can i use a cauldron to store lava and does it do anything special
answer: Yes, you can store lava in a cauldron by using a lava bucket on it. A lava-filled cauldron emits a light level of 15 and outputs a redstone signal strength of 3 when a comparator is placed behind it.

question: if i put a cauldron under pointed dripstone will it fill up with water or lava
answer: Yes, a cauldron placed up to 10 blocks below a downward-facing pointed dripstone will slowly fill with the liquid source placed a block above the dripstone. For this to work, there must be no solid blocks, water, or lava between the dripstone and the cauldron, and the dripstone must have a waterlogged block or water source (for water) or a lava source (for lava) directly above it.

question: can i put silk touch on my sword to get mob heads or something?
answer: No, you cannot apply Silk Touch to swords in normal gameplay. It is only meant for tools like pickaxes, shovels, axes, and hoes.

question: what happens if i have both silk touch and fortune on a pickaxe?
answer: Silk Touch and Fortune are incompatible enchantments. If you use commands to force both onto one item, Silk Touch will take precedence, meaning you will get the block itself instead of the extra drops Fortune would have provided.

question: why does my stone pickaxe with silk touch still give me cobbled deepslate instead of the deepslate block?
answer: Deepslate is a special case where it only drops itself if you mine it with a pickaxe enchanted with Silk Touch. If you mine it with any pickaxe that lacks that enchantment, it will always drop cobbled deepslate regardless of the material of the pickaxe you are using.

question: how do i craft shears in minecraft
answer: You can craft shears by placing two iron ingots in a crafting grid.

question: do shears lose durability if i try to shear a sheep that has no wool
answer: No, shears do not lose durability if the shearing attempt fails. Durability is only consumed when the action is successful, such as when you successfully harvest wool from a sheep.

question: what happens to bees if i use a dispenser to harvest honeycombs from a nest
answer: When a dispenser is used to shear a beehive or bee nest, it does not anger the bees or cause them to leave the hive, even if there is no campfire placed underneath. This is different from shearing by hand, which will cause all bees inside to exit and attack you if you do not have a campfire or soul campfire below the hive to smoke them out.

question: how do i make a block of diamond
answer: You can craft a block of diamond by placing 9 diamonds in a 3x3 grid on a crafting table.

question: what happens if i use silk touch on diamond ore
answer: If you mine diamond ore with a pickaxe enchanted with Silk Touch, it will drop the ore block itself instead of a diamond. You would then need to smelt that ore block to get the diamond.

question: is it worth using fortune 3 on diamond ore
answer: Yes, using Fortune III is highly recommended because it increases the yield significantly. While a standard diamond ore block drops only one diamond, Fortune III allows for a chance to drop extra diamonds per level, resulting in a maximum of 4 diamonds from a single ore block. This makes it the most efficient way to gather diamonds when mining.

question: how do i make a new project in the bedrock editor
answer: You can create a new project directly from the main menu of the Bedrock Editor. Once you are in the projects list, simply select the option to create a new project to get started.

question: can i use the editor on my server and what happens to my world
answer: Yes, you can enable the Editor for a Bedrock Dedicated Server by running the server with the 'Editor=true' command. Note that this only works if the server does not already contain any worlds, and only players using an Editor client will be able to join to work on the project.

question: why are my achievements off when i use the editor
answer: Achievements are always disabled for any projects or worlds created using the Bedrock Editor. This is because a project is not considered a standard 'played' world until it is exported, and it remains in an initial creation state during the editing process. Even after you export the project as a playable world file, the achievement system remains inactive for that specific file.

question: how do i get milk from a cow
answer: You can milk an adult cow by using a bucket on it. This will give you a milk bucket.

question: why wont my baby cow grow up
answer: If you fed the baby cow a golden dandelion, its growth process is frozen and reset to 20 minutes. You need to feed it another golden dandelion to resume its growth.

question: do cows spawn as different types depending on where i find them
answer: Yes, cows have three variants—temperate, cold, and warm—which are determined by the biome they spawn in. For example, cows in plains or forests are temperate, while those in snowy taigas are cold and those in jungles or badlands are warm. If you spawn a cow using a spawn egg or command in a biome not listed in the variant table, it will default to the temperate variant.

question: how long do items stay on the ground before they disappear?
answer: Dropped items that do not become blocks will remain for 5 minutes in a loaded chunk before they despawn.

question: why do some of my items stack to 16 while others go to 64?
answer: Most full blocks stack up to 64, but certain items like snowballs, eggs, signs, and ender pearls are limited to a stack size of 16. Items that have durability, such as tools, weapons, and armor, do not stack at all.

question: how do i make an item frame to display my stuff?
answer: You cannot craft an item frame in survival mode using standard recipes, but you can obtain them to display items on walls or shelves. Once you have one, you place it on a block and then use an item on it to mount the object inside the frame for display.

question: how do i get a grass block in my inventory?
answer: You can obtain a grass block by mining it with a tool enchanted with Silk Touch. Alternatively, you can kill an enderman that is holding a grass block, and it will drop the block.

question: why does my grass keep turning into dirt?
answer: Grass dies and turns into dirt if it is covered by an opaque block, or if a transparent block reduces the light level above the grass to four or lower. If the area is not sufficiently illuminated, the grass will eventually die when a random tick lands on the block.

question: what are the specific rules for grass spreading to nearby dirt?
answer: For grass to spread, the target dirt block must be within a 3x5x3 range of the source grass block, with the source block in the center of the second topmost layer. The source grass must have a light level of 9 or brighter directly above it, and the block above the target dirt must allow light to pass through. Additionally, the block directly above the target dirt cannot be water or lava, though other light-filtering blocks like ice or glass do not prevent the spread.

question: how do i craft a bow in minecraft?
answer: you can craft a bow by placing three sticks and three strings in a crafting table.

question: can i put mending and infinity on the same bow?
answer: no, you cannot have both mending and infinity on the same bow. these two enchantments are mutually exclusive, so you have to choose one or the other.

question: why does my bow shake when i hold it drawn for a long time?
answer: if you are playing in a combat test version, this is called bow fatigue. after holding the bow fully drawn for more than 3 seconds, the bow begins to shake violently, which increases your inaccuracy and causes you to lose the critical charge bonus.

question: what do i feed a cat to tame it?
answer: You can tame a stray cat by feeding it raw cod or raw salmon. Each fish has a 1/3 chance of successfully taming the cat.

question: why wont my cat stop sitting on my chest?
answer: Tamed cats that are not currently sitting will often try to sit on chests, beds, or active furnaces within a 4-block radius. To make the cat move, you can command it to stand, remove the block it is sitting on, or push it off.

question: do cats teleport if they are sitting down?
answer: Generally, a cat does not teleport if it has been ordered to sit. However, there is an exception: if the cat is injured while sitting, it is likely to teleport to its owner to escape the danger. Once it teleports, it will no longer be in the sitting position.

question: how do i make a map in minecraft
answer: You can craft a map by placing paper in a crafting table. In Bedrock Edition, you can also use a cartography table with paper to create an empty map.

question: why is my map not showing my player icon
answer: If you are playing in Bedrock Edition, you need to use a locator map to see your position marker. If you have a regular map, you can turn it into a locator map by combining it with a compass in a crafting grid, anvil, or cartography table.

question: do maps work in the nether or the end
answer: Maps function differently depending on the dimension. In the End, they work as usual to map terrain and show your position. In the Nether, maps are unreliable; they show a red-and-gray pattern instead of terrain, and your player pointer will spin rapidly, making it difficult to navigate. While they aren't great for mapping the area, they can still be useful for tracking your location relative to framed copies of the same map.

question: what animals only spawn in the jungle biome?
answer: Parrots, ocelots, and pandas are the animals that spawn exclusively in jungle biomes.

question: can i find jungle temples in every type of jungle?
answer: No, jungle pyramids are exclusive to jungle biomes but they only generate in regular jungles and bamboo jungles. Note that in Java Edition, they specifically generate in bamboo jungles, while in other versions, the generation rules may differ.

question: why does my game lag so much when i walk through a jungle?
answer: Jungle biomes are significantly more resource-demanding to load and render compared to other biomes because of the dense vegetation and large trees. The game has to process a high volume of foliage, vines, and tall jungle trees, which can cause frame rate drops. If you are experiencing performance issues, you may want to turn down your graphics settings while exploring these areas.

question: do husks burn in the sun like normal zombies?
answer: No, husks are immune to burning in sunlight, which allows them to stay active during the day.

question: why do i get hungry when fighting husks?
answer: Husks apply the Hunger effect to players or mobs they hit with their unarmed attacks. This effect lasts for 7 multiplied by the floor of your regional difficulty in seconds, though it won't apply if you block the attack with a shield or if the husk is holding an item.

question: what happens to a husk if it stays underwater for a long time?
answer: If a husk is fully submerged in water for 30 seconds, it begins a conversion process that takes an additional 15 seconds to complete. Once finished, the husk turns into a normal zombie with full health, regardless of how much damage the husk had taken previously. If it remains submerged after this transformation, it may eventually convert further into a drowned.

question: how do i craft a block of redstone
answer: You can craft a block of redstone by filling a crafting grid with 9 redstone dust.

question: can i use silk touch on redstone ore to get the dust
answer: No, if you mine redstone ore with a silk touch pickaxe, the block drops itself instead of redstone dust. To get the dust, you need to mine it with an iron pickaxe or higher without silk touch.

question: why does my redstone wire stay powered when it should be off
answer: Redstone wire does not always update surrounding components when it reconfigures after placement. If the wire changes shape or connection, the nearby mechanism might stay activated until it receives a separate update from another source. This behavior is often used to create a block update detector.

question: how do i find a stronghold in my world?
answer: You can locate a stronghold by using an eye of ender. It will lead you to the underground structure where the End portal is hidden.

question: are there more strongholds in bedrock than in java edition?
answer: Yes, in Bedrock Edition there is no limit to the number of strongholds that can generate in a world. In Java Edition, however, there are exactly 128 strongholds total, arranged in specific rings around the world center.

question: why did my portal room not spawn in the stronghold i found?
answer: The stronghold generation process is random and can extend up to 50 rooms away from the start. If a portal room fails to generate during that process, the entire generation for that structure restarts, so it is possible to find a stronghold that seems incomplete or lacks the portal room entirely. You should keep exploring or look for a different stronghold, as the portal room is the only part of the structure guaranteed to exist in every successfully formed stronghold.

question: how do i make a crafting table
answer: You can craft a crafting table by placing four wooden planks in the 2x2 crafting grid found in your inventory screen.

question: can i flip recipes in the crafting grid
answer: Yes, you can flip shaped recipes horizontally, but not vertically. Note that some fixed recipes cannot be moved or mirrored at all.

question: how do hoppers interact with the crafter
answer: Hoppers can automatically input items into a crafter to fill enabled slots. Items fill these slots equally from left to right and top to bottom in that specific order. If you disable all slots, hoppers will be unable to put any items into the crafter.

question: how do i make tnt in minecraft
answer: You can craft TNT by combining 5 gunpowder and 4 sand or red sand in a crafting grid. You can use any combination of regular sand and red sand to make it.

question: does tnt explode if i light it while sneaking
answer: If you try to ignite TNT while sneaking, the block will catch fire instead of becoming primed immediately. This allows you to place it without it starting the countdown right away.

question: why does my tnt not break blocks when i use it underwater
answer: In most cases, TNT cannot destroy blocks underwater because the water prevents the explosion from affecting the surrounding area. However, in Java Edition, you can bypass this by placing a gravity-affected block like sand or gravel on top of the TNT before igniting it, or by placing the TNT on a block that is less than a full block in height, such as soul sand. These methods allow the explosion to function even when submerged.

question: how do i make coarse dirt
answer: You can craft coarse dirt by combining two blocks of dirt and two blocks of gravel in a crafting grid.

question: what happens to farmland if i dont plant anything on it
answer: Farmland will turn back into dirt if nothing is planted on it and it is not within four blocks of water. It can also revert if a mob jumps on it or if you place a solid block on top of it.

question: is there a way to get infinite dirt without just digging up the whole world
answer: Yes, you can make dirt renewable by using moss blocks. By tilling coarse dirt, you can turn gravel into dirt, and since gravel is renewable through bartering with piglins, this provides a steady supply. Alternatively, you can use bone meal to turn stone into moss, then use azalea trees or saplings to convert that moss into rooted dirt or regular dirt depending on your game version.

question: how do i make a sword in minecraft?
answer: You can craft a sword by placing one stick and two of the tier's material (like planks, iron ingots, or diamonds) in a crafting table.

question: does using a sword to break blocks hurt its durability?
answer: Yes, using a sword to break any block that doesn't break instantly by hand decreases its durability by 2. This applies even to bamboo, which the sword breaks instantly.

question: why did my sweep attack not hit the mobs around my target?
answer: In Java Edition, a sweep attack only triggers if you are on the ground, not sprinting, and your attack recharge meter is at least 84.8% full. Additionally, the surrounding enemies must be within a 1x0.25x1 block area of the mob you hit, and their feet must be within 3 blocks of your own feet for the gale particle to affect them.

question: how do i get a spawn egg in survival mode?
answer: you actually cannot obtain spawn eggs in survival mode. they are only available through the creative menu or by using commands.

question: if i use a spawn egg on a mob, will it always spawn a baby?
answer: not always. using a spawn egg on a mob that has a baby form will spawn a baby version, but this does not work for camel husks. additionally, many mobs have a specific percentage chance to spawn as a baby when you use the egg on a surface.

question: why do my mobs keep disappearing when i use spawn eggs?
answer: if you are playing on peaceful difficulty, most monster mobs spawned with an egg are created and then immediately deleted from the world. the exceptions to this are undead animals, shulkers, piglins, hoglins, and ender dragons, which will stay. also, if you rename a spawn egg on an anvil, the resulting mob will have that name, but that does not prevent hostile mobs from despawning like a name tag would.

question: what does the luck attribute actually do in minecraft?
answer: The luck attribute affects the results of loot tables when you are fishing, opening chests, or killing mobs. It specifically influences the quality or bonus_rolls tags to potentially improve your loot.

question: how do i make a zombie that follows me from further away?
answer: You can change how far a mob tracks you by modifying its follow range attribute. When summoning a zombie, you can use the command /summon zombie ~ ~ ~ {attributes:[{id:"follow_range", base:100.0d}]} to make it follow you from 100 blocks away instead of the default 35.

question: if i have two different modifiers for the same attribute, do they just add up?
answer: It depends on the operation used by the modifiers. The game processes them in a specific order: first it applies all add_value modifiers, then all add_multiplied_base modifiers, and finally all add_multiplied_total modifiers. The total value calculated after each step is carried over as the base for the next set of operations, so they do not simply stack together in one flat sum.

question: do ghasts drop anything when they die?
answer: Yes, when killed, ghasts drop ghast tears, gunpowder, and a music disc called 'Tears'.

question: can i use smite on a ghast to kill it faster?
answer: No, smite does not work on ghasts because they are not considered undead mobs. Even though they look like ghosts, they can drown, be healed by instant health potions, and are affected by poison.

question: why do ghasts sometimes ignore me even when i am right in front of them?
answer: Ghasts require a clear line of sight to target you, which can be blocked by any solid or transparent block. In Java Edition, they only target players within 64 blocks horizontally and 4 blocks vertically. Additionally, they are completely ignored by several other mobs like wolves, zoglins, and snow golems, so they might be focused on something else or simply unable to see you through the environment.

question: how do i get glass in minecraft
answer: You can obtain glass by smelting sand or red sand in a furnace using any fuel. You can also trade with librarian villagers to get it.

question: can i pick up glass after i place it down
answer: If you break glass normally, it drops nothing. To get the block back, you must use a tool enchanted with Silk Touch.

question: why do my redstone signals act weird when i run them over glass
answer: Glass behaves like a top slab in redstone circuits. While you can place redstone dust and components on top of it, the glass itself cannot be powered. Additionally, in Java Edition, glass will transmit redstone signals upward but it will not transmit them downward.

question: how do i breed rabbits in minecraft
answer: You can breed rabbits by feeding them carrots, golden carrots, or dandelions.

question: do rabbits run away from all mobs
answer: Rabbits avoid most monsters within 4 blocks and wolves within 10 blocks, but they do not try to avoid foxes or cats unless they are actively being attacked by them.

question: why do my baby rabbits take so long to grow up
answer: Baby rabbits take 20 minutes to reach adulthood naturally. You can speed this up by feeding them carrots, golden carrots, or dandelions, which each reduce the remaining growth time by 10%. Note that if you use golden dandelions, they will stop the baby from growing up entirely.

question: how do i put a chest on my donkey?
answer: You can equip a chest to a tamed donkey by holding the chest and using it on the donkey. Once equipped, it provides 15 inventory slots for your items.

question: can i breed a donkey with a horse and what happens?
answer: Yes, you can breed a donkey with a horse to create a mule. Keep in mind that mules are sterile and cannot breed with other animals.

question: why is my donkey taking so much fall damage when i ride it?
answer: Donkeys are actually quite resilient to falls, as they can safely drop 6 blocks before taking any damage, which is double the distance of most other mobs. When you are riding one, you share that same fall damage protection. However, they still take damage if the fall is higher than 6 blocks, and they take half the normal damage amount for every block beyond that safe distance.

question: what animals can i find in a desert?
answer: In the wild, you can find rabbits and camels. Desert villages also contain cats, cows, sheep, pigs, and camels.

question: why are there so many zombies and skeletons in my desert at daytime?
answer: In deserts, most zombies are replaced by husks and most skeletons are replaced by parched. Unlike regular zombies and skeletons, these variants do not burn in the sun, which is why you see them during the day.

question: i'm stuck in a desert with no trees, how am i supposed to get wood?
answer: Deserts are very barren and do not have natural trees. To get wood, you must locate a pillager outpost, which is the only source of wood on the surface. Alternatively, you can search for mineshafts underground or find azalea trees if there happens to be a lush cave beneath the desert.

question: how do i get shulker shells?
answer: You get shulker shells by killing shulkers, which are found in End cities. They have a 50% base chance to drop a shell when killed.

question: do shulkers despawn if i leave them in the end?
answer: No, shulkers do not despawn naturally, even if you are playing on Peaceful difficulty. However, they will stop attacking you if you are in Peaceful mode.

question: why do my shulkers keep disappearing or teleporting when i try to farm them?
answer: Shulkers teleport if they cannot attach to an adjacent full block or if they take damage while below half health. If you are trying to farm them by hitting them with their own bullets, remember that they only duplicate if the hit shulker is open, survives the damage, and has enough space to teleport. Also, the chance to duplicate decreases by 20% for every other shulker within 8 blocks, so if there are 5 or more nearby, they will never duplicate.

question: how do i make a block of coal
answer: You can craft a block of coal by placing 9 pieces of coal into a crafting grid.

question: is it better to use coal or coal blocks for smelting
answer: When you need to smelt a large amount of items, it is more efficient to use a block of coal. A single block of coal is crafted from 9 pieces of coal, but it lasts 10 times longer as fuel than 9 individual pieces of coal would.

question: does fortune iii actually give me 4 coal from one ore block
answer: Yes, if you mine coal ore with a pickaxe enchanted with Fortune III, you can get up to 4 pieces of coal from a single block. The enchantment allows for an extra piece of coal per level of Fortune, and since the base drop is 1, the maximum yield is 4.

question: how do i open the advancements screen in game?
answer: You can open the advancements screen by pressing the L key on your keyboard, or by accessing it through the pause menu.

question: do i get a notification every time i complete an advancement?
answer: You will see a sliding toast notification in the top right corner and receive a chat message for most advancements, provided the show_advancement_messages game rule is enabled. However, the five root advancements for each tab do not trigger any chat messages or notifications.

question: why cant i see the how did we get here advancement in my menu?
answer: This is a hidden advancement, which means it remains invisible in your interface until you have actually completed it.
